originContent: Built with ❤️ for all Astro crewmates 🧑‍🚀
translatedContent: Built with ❤️ for all Astro crewmates 🧑‍🚀

originContent: Provide an internationalization (i18n) integration for Astro that:
translatedContent: Provide an internationalization (i18n) integration for Astro that:

originContent: en: "en-US", // the `defaultLocale` value must present in `locales` keys
translatedContent: en: "en-US", // the `defaultLocale` value must present in `locales` keys

originContent: Now that you have set up the config, each `.astro` page will have additional renders with your other languages. For example, `src/pages/about.astro` will render as:
translatedContent: Now that you have set up the config, each `.astro` page will have additional renders with your other languages. For example, `src/pages/about.astro` will render as:

originContent: If you have enabled `redirectDefaultLocale` (`true` by default), redirects will be:
translatedContent: If you have enabled `redirectDefaultLocale` (`true` by default), redirects will be:

originContent: Please note that the `getStaticPaths()` function will only run once. This limitation means that you cannot have translated urls, such as `/es/acerca-de/` for `/about/`. However, it also ensures compatibility with [`@astrojs/sitemap`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@astrojs/sitemap).
translatedContent: Please note that the `getStaticPaths()` function will only run once. This limitation means that you cannot have translated urls, such as `/es/acerca-de/` for `/about/`. However, it also ensures compatibility with [`@astrojs/sitemap`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@astrojs/sitemap).

originContent: The Astro frontmatter and page content is re-run for every translated page. For example, the `Astro.url.pathname` will be:
translatedContent: The Astro frontmatter and page content is re-run for every translated page. For example, the `Astro.url.pathname` will be:

originContent: It is up to you to detect which language is being rendered. You can use Astro [content collections](https://docs.astro.build/en/guides/content-collections/) or any i18n UI framework, such as [`react-i18next`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/react-i18next), for your translations. Here is a pure `Hello World` example:
translatedContent: It is up to you to detect which language is being rendered. You can use Astro [content collections](https://docs.astro.build/en/guides/content-collections/) or any i18n UI framework, such as [`react-i18next`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/react-i18next), for your translations. Here is a pure `Hello World` example:

originContent: Several helper functions are included to make handling locales easier.
translatedContent: Several helper functions are included to make handling locales easier.

originContent: Please see the official Astro docs for more details:
translatedContent: Please see the official Astro docs for more details:
All these options are related and must be set together. They affect whether your urls are:

originContent: All these options are related and must be set together. They affect whether your urls are:

translatedContent: If you choose `/about/`, then `/about` will 404 and vice versa.

originContent: If you choose `/about/`, then `/about` will 404 and vice versa.

translatedContent: - `locales`: A record of all language locales.

originContent: - `locales`: A record of all language locales.
translatedContent: - `defaultLocale`: The default language locale. The value must present in `locales` keys.

originContent: - `defaultLocale`: The default language locale. The value must present in `locales` keys.

translatedContent: cannot be translated. If you choose to use them in the `pages` directory, please add them to the ignore glob patterns. For example:

originContent: cannot be translated. If you choose to use them in the `pages` directory, please add them to the ignore glob patterns. For example:

> You can exclude pages or directories from being built by prefixing their names with an underscore (_). Files with the _ prefix won’t be recognized by the router and won’t be placed into the dist/ directory.
translatedContent: > You can exclude pages or directories from being built by prefixing their names with an underscore (_). Files with the _ prefix won’t be recognized by the router and won’t be placed into the dist/ directory.

originContent: > You can use this to temporarily disable pages, and also to put tests, utilities, and components in the same folder as their related pages.
translatedContent: > You can use this to temporarily disable pages, and also to put tests, utilities, and components in the same folder as their related pages.

originContent: Unfortunately, this [excluding pages](https://docs.astro.build/en/core-concepts/routing/#excluding-pages) feature is not supported. Please only keep pages in your pages directory.
translatedContent: Unfortunately, this [excluding pages](https://docs.astro.build/en/core-concepts/routing/#excluding-pages) feature is not supported. Please only keep pages in your pages directory.

originContent: You can still exclude pages prefixed with an underscore (`_`) by adding `pages/**/_*` to the ignore glob patterns:
translatedContent: You can still exclude pages prefixed with an underscore (`_`) by adding `pages/**/_*` to the ignore glob patterns:

originContent: With this library and Astro Content Collections, you can keep your Markdown separate and organized in `content`, while using `pages/blog/index.astro` and `pages/blog/[slug].astro` to render all of your content, even with a `defaultLocale`! Here is an example folder structure:
translatedContent: With this library and Astro Content Collections, you can keep your Markdown separate and organized in `content`, while using `pages/blog/index.astro` and `pages/blog/[slug].astro` to render all of your content, even with a `defaultLocale`! Here is an example folder structure:

originContent: Astro does not support `.tsx` or `.jsx` as page file types.
translatedContent: Astro does not support `.tsx` or `.jsx` as page file types.

originContent: For UI frameworks like React and Vue, use them how you [normally](https://docs.astro.build/en/core-concepts/framework-components/) would with Astro by importing them as components.
translatedContent: For UI frameworks like React and Vue, use them how you [normally](https://docs.astro.build/en/core-concepts/framework-components/) would with Astro by importing them as components.

originContent: Feel free to pass the translated content `title={t('title')}` or locale `locale={locale}` as props.
translatedContent: Feel free to pass the translated content `title={t('title')}` or locale `locale={locale}` as props.

originContent: By default, all pages in `pages/api/**/*` are ignored.
translatedContent: By default, all pages in `pages/api/**/*` are ignored.

originContent: For `.ts` and `.js` endpoints, how you handle multiple locales is up to you. As endpoints are not user-facing and there are many different ways to use endpoints, we leave the implementation up to your preferences.
translatedContent: For `.ts` and `.js` endpoints, how you handle multiple locales is up to you. As endpoints are not user-facing and there are many different ways to use endpoints, we leave the implementation up to your preferences.

originContent: PRs welcome! Thank you for your help. Read more in the [contributing guide](https://github.com/jlarmstrongiv/astro-i18n-aut/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for reporting bugs and making PRs.
translatedContent: PRs welcome! Thank you for your help. Read more in the [contributing guide](https://github.com/jlarmstrongiv/astro-i18n-aut/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) for reporting bugs and making PRs.
